chapters.title: Real Numbers
topics: Euclid's Division Algorithm to find HCF
Prime Factorisation method for HCF & LCM
formulas: HCF × LCM = Product of two numbers
a = bq + r, where 0 ≤ r < b (Euclid's Division Lemma)
If p divides a², then p divides a (Theorem)
importantQuestions: Prove that √2 is irrational
Prove that √3 is irrational
Find HCF and LCM of 306 and 657 using Euclid's algorithm
Show that 5 - √3 is irrational
Find HCF of 96 and 404 by prime factorisation
tips: HCF/LCM ke sawaal mein pehle prime factorisation karo — easy and quick!
Irrational proof mein contradiction method zaroori hai — assume rational, then show contradiction
Decimal expansion check karne ke liye denominator ko 2ⁿ × 5ᵐ form mein likho

chapters.title: Polynomials
topics: Geometrical meaning of zeroes of a polynomial
Relationship between zeroes and coefficients — Linear, Quadratic, Cubic
formulas: For ax² + bx + c: Sum of zeroes (α+β) = -b/a
For ax² + bx + c: Product of zeroes (αβ) = c/a
For ax³ + bx² + cx + d: α+β+γ = -b/a
For cubic: αβ + βγ + γα = c/a
p(x) = g(x) × q(x) + r(x) — Division Algorithm
importantQuestions: Find zeroes of 6x² - 3 - 7x and verify the relationship
If α and β are zeroes of x² - 6x + a, find 'a' if 3α + 2β = 20
Find a quadratic polynomial whose sum and product of zeroes are -3 and 2
Divide 3x² - x³ - 3x + 5 by x - 1 - x²
tips: Quadratic ke zeroes nikaalne ke liye factorisation ya splitting the middle term use karo
Relationship verify karna mat bhoolna — exam mein marks katne ka common reason
Cubic polynomial mein teen relationships hain — teeno likho

chapters.title: Pair of Linear Equations in Two Variables
topics: Graphical method of solving linear equations
Conditions for unique, infinite, or no solution
Word problems based on linear equations
formulas: a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ ≠ c₁/c₂ → No solution (Parallel lines)
a₁/a₂ = b₁/b₂ = c₁/c₂ → Infinite solutions (Coincident lines)
importantQuestions: Solve: 2x + 3y = 11 and 2x - 4y = -24
For what value of k: 2x + 3y = 7 and (k-1)x + (k+2)y = 3k has infinite solutions?
The sum of digits of a 2-digit number is 9. Number obtained by reversing digits is 27 more. Find the number.
5 pencils and 7 pens cost ₹50; 7 pencils and 5 pens cost ₹46. Find cost of each.
tips: Word problems mein variables clearly define karo — x = kya, y = kya likhna zaroori hai
Elimination method sabse fast hai zyaadatar questions ke liye
Graphical method mein at least 3 points plot karo — accuracy ke liye
Conditions (a₁/a₂, b₁/b₂, c₁/c₂) ratta lagao — direct 1-2 marks ka sawaal aata hai

chapters.title: Quadratic Equations
topics: Word problems based on quadratic equations
importantQuestions: Solve: 2x² - 7x + 3 = 0 by factorisation
Find the nature of roots of 2x² - 4x + 3 = 0
The altitude of a right triangle is 7 cm less than its base. If hypotenuse is 13 cm, find the other sides.
A train travels 480 km at uniform speed. If speed had been 8 km/h less, it would have taken 3 hours more. Find speed.
Find two consecutive positive integers whose sum of squares is 365.
tips: Discriminant question bahut easy marks hai — D calculate karo aur nature likho
Speed-distance-time problems mein table banao — easy ho jaata hai
Factorisation mushkil lage toh seedha formula use karo — galat nahi hai
Word problems mein negative root reject karna mat bhoolna (agar applicable ho)

chapters.title: Arithmetic Progressions (AP)
topics: Sum of first n terms of an AP (Sₙ)
Finding AP when sum is given
formulas: Sum of n terms: Sₙ = n/2 [2a + (n-1)d]
Also: Sₙ = n/2 [a + l] where l = last term
Common difference: d = a₂ - a₁ = a₃ - a₂
importantQuestions: Find the 31st term of AP: 5, 11, 17, 23, ...
How many terms of AP 24, 21, 18, ... must be taken so that their sum is 78?
If sum of 1st 7 terms = 49 and sum of 17 terms = 289, find sum of n terms.
Find the sum of first 40 positive integers divisible by 6.
The sum of 3rd and 7th terms of an AP is 6 and their product is 8. Find the sum of first 16 terms.
tips: aₙ = Sₙ - Sₙ₋₁ yaad rakho — jab sum diya ho aur particular term poocha jaaye
3 numbers in AP: a-d, a, a+d le lo (sum simple ho jaata hai)
4 numbers in AP: a-3d, a-d, a+d, a+3d (common difference 2d)
Sum of natural numbers = n(n+1)/2 — yeh bhi kaam aata hai

chapters.title: Triangles
formulas: BPT: If DE ∥ BC in △ABC, then AD/DB = AE/EC
Ratio of areas of similar triangles = (ratio of sides)²
Pythagoras: AC² = AB² + BC² (right angle at B)
If ratio of sides = k, ratio of areas = k²
importantQuestions: Prove Basic Proportionality Theorem (BPT) — complete proof
Prove Pythagoras Theorem — complete proof
In △ABC, DE ∥ BC with AD = 4cm, DB = 5cm, AE = 8cm. Find EC.
Sides of two similar triangles are in ratio 4:9. Find ratio of their areas.
A vertical pole of length 6m casts a shadow 4m. Find height of tower casting 28m shadow.
tips: BPT proof aur Pythagoras proof ratta lagao — yeh guaranteed 4-5 marks ka question hai
Similar triangles mein corresponding sides match karna important hai — order galat mat karo
Area ratio = (side ratio)² — yeh formula direct apply hota hai

chapters.title: Coordinate Geometry
importantQuestions: Find the distance between (-1, 7) and (4, -3)
Find the point on x-axis equidistant from (2, -5) and (-2, 9)
Find the ratio in which (4, -1) divides the join of (1, -7) and (8, 9)
Find area of triangle with vertices (2, 3), (4, 7), (-2, 2)
Show that points (1, -1), (5, 2), (9, 5) are collinear
tips: Distance formula mein negative signs ka dhyaan rakho — (x₂-x₁)² always positive hoga
Area of triangle ka value 0 aaye toh points collinear hain — directly likh do
Section formula mein m:n ka ratio clearly identify karo — ulta mat karo

chapters.title: Introduction to Trigonometry
topics: Trigonometric ratios of an acute angle
formulas: tan θ = Opposite/Adjacent = P/B = sin θ/cos θ
cosec θ = 1/sin θ, sec θ = 1/cos θ, cot θ = 1/tan θ
sin(90°-θ) = cos θ, cos(90°-θ) = sin θ
tan(90°-θ) = cot θ, cot(90°-θ) = tan θ
importantQuestions: If tan A = 4/3, find all other trigonometric ratios
Evaluate: sin 60° cos 30° + sin 30° cos 60°
Prove: (1 + tan²A)/(1 + cot²A) = tan²A
Prove: (sin θ - cos θ + 1)/(sin θ + cos θ - 1) = 1/(sec θ - tan θ)
If sin A = 3/4, find cos A and tan A
tips: sin-cos-tan table (0° to 90°) ratta lagao — har paper mein aata hai
SOH-CAH-TOA yaad rakho: Sin=O/H, Cos=A/H, Tan=O/A
Proving identities mein LHS se start karo, RHS ke taraf jaao
Complementary angle sawaal mein (90°-θ) replace karo — simple ho jaata hai

chapters.title: Some Applications of Trigonometry
formulas: Angle of elevation: angle from horizontal UP to object
Angle of depression: angle from horizontal DOWN to object
Angle of depression from top = Angle of elevation from bottom
For two objects: Use two right triangles with common side
importantQuestions: From the top of a 7m building, angle of elevation of a tower is 60° and depression of its foot is 45°. Find height of tower.
A kite is flying at 75m height. String makes 60° with ground. Find length of string.
A 1.5m tall boy sees the top of a 30m high building at elevation of 30°. Find distance.
From a point on ground, angles of elevation of top and bottom of a flagstaff on building are 60° and 45°. Find heights.
tips: Figure draw karna zaroori hai — 90% students galat karte hain bina figure ke
Angle of depression aur elevation mein confuse mat ho — figure mein clearly mark karo
√3 = 1.732, √2 = 1.414 approximate values yaad rakho
Hamesha right triangle banao — tan ratio sabse zyada use hota hai

chapters.title: Circles
topics: Number of tangents from a point
Theorem: Tangent perpendicular to radius at point of contact
Theorem: Lengths of tangents from external point are equal
formulas: Tangent ⊥ Radius at point of contact (OA ⊥ PA)
PA = PB (tangents from external point P)
If ∠OPA = θ, then ∠OPB = θ (tangents equally inclined to line joining centre and external point)
In circumscribed quadrilateral: AB + CD = BC + DA
importantQuestions: Prove that the tangent at any point of a circle is perpendicular to the radius — PROOF
Prove that lengths of tangents drawn from external point are equal — PROOF
Two concentric circles have radii 5cm and 3cm. Find length of chord of larger circle touching smaller circle.
PQ is a tangent to a circle with centre O at point P. If △OPQ is isosceles, find ∠OQP.
Prove that the parallelogram circumscribing a circle is a rhombus.
tips: Dono theorems ke proofs ratto — exam mein guaranteed 3-4 marks
Tangent problems mein Pythagoras Theorem bhi lagta hai — triangle banake dekho
Circumscribed circle mein tangent lengths equal hoti hain — yeh property key hai

chapters.title: Constructions
topics: Division of a line segment in given ratio
Construction of triangle similar to given triangle (scale factor)
Construction of tangents to a circle from external point
Construction of tangent at a point on the circle
formulas: For tangent from external point: Join centre to external point, draw perpendicular bisector
importantQuestions: Draw a triangle ABC with BC=6cm, AB=5cm, AC=4cm. Construct a similar triangle with sides 3/4 of corresponding sides.
Draw a line segment of 7cm and divide it in ratio 2:3.
Draw a circle of radius 3cm. From a point 7cm away from centre, construct pair of tangents.
Construct a triangle with sides 5cm, 6cm, 7cm. Then construct a triangle similar to it with scale factor 5/3.
tips: Scale factor ka ratio dhyaan se padho — 3/4 means chhota triangle, 7/5 means bada
Construction mein pencil sharp rakho aur arcs clearly draw karo
Steps likho construction ke saath — marks aate hain steps ke liye bhi
Tangent construction mein semicircle wala method use karo — sabse reliable

chapters.title: Areas Related to Circles
topics: Perimeter and area of a circle — revision
Area of sector of a circle
Area of segment of a circle
Areas of combinations of plane figures
formulas: Area of segment = Area of sector - Area of △
For major segment: πr² - area of minor segment
importantQuestions: Find the area of a sector of angle 120° of a circle of radius 21 cm.
A chord of a circle of radius 15cm subtends 60° at centre. Find area of minor and major segments. (Use π=3.14, √3=1.73)
Find the area of shaded region where ABCD is a square of side 14cm with four equal semicircles.
Find the area of quadrant of a circle whose circumference is 22 cm.
tips: π = 22/7 ya 3.14 question mein dekho — jo diya ho wahi use karo
Combination figures mein areas add/subtract karo methodically
Segment = Sector - Triangle — yeh formula key hai
Quadrant = sector with θ = 90°

chapters.title: Surface Areas and Volumes
topics: Surface area of combination of solids
Conversion of one shape to another
formulas: When solid converted: Volume remains same
importantQuestions: A metallic sphere of radius 4.2 cm is melted and recast into shape of a cylinder of radius 6 cm. Find height.
A tent is in shape of cylinder surmounted by a cone. If height of cylinder is 2.1m, and slant height of cone is 2.8m, radius is 2m, find area of canvas.
A solid is in form of cone mounted on a hemisphere. Find total surface area if radius = 1 cm, height of cone = 1 cm.
Water in a cylindrical vessel of radius 12cm rises by 3.5cm when a solid sphere is immersed. Find radius of sphere.
tips: Combination mein overlapping surface area subtract karo — common galti yahi hoti hai
Melting/recasting mein volume same rehta hai — equate volumes
Frustum ke sawaal mein r₁ aur r₂ identify karo carefully
Units check karo — cm ya m mixed na ho

chapters.title: Probability
topics: Probability of impossible and sure events
Problems on coins, dice, cards, and balls
formulas: P(E) = Number of favourable outcomes / Total outcomes
One die: 6 outcomes | Two dice: 36 outcomes
One coin: 2 outcomes | Two coins: 4 outcomes
Pack of cards: 52 total = 4 suits × 13 cards
importantQuestions: Two dice are thrown together. Find probability of getting: (i) same number (ii) sum = 8 (iii) doublet of even numbers
A box has 12 balls: 5 red, 4 blue, 3 green. Find P(not green).
A card is drawn from a well-shuffled deck. Find P(king), P(red queen), P(face card), P(not diamond)
A bag contains 5 red and some black balls. If P(black) = 3 × P(red), find number of black balls.
1500 families surveyed for number of children. Make probability distribution.
tips: Cards ke sawaal mein: 52 cards, 4 suits (♠♥♦♣), 13 each, 12 face cards, 4 aces — yeh ratta lagao
Two dice mein 36 outcomes hain — table banaake dekho
P(not E) = 1 - P(E) ka use karo jab 'not' waala poochhein — seedha kaam banta hai
Sample space pehle likho — phir favourable count karo — mistake nahi hogi
